notes: Converted from the old chapter extraction schema.
Hidden facts, motives, and faction pressures require living-world re-extraction.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

The seed package describes an open scene where the Four Lechers of Central Continent capture female Gu Masters, attempt to assault them, and are stopped by Zhao Lianyun who uses illusion punishment. Most events are publicly visible (battle, capture, attempted assault, rescue). No private knowledge, secret plans, or future-known information is directly supported by the source text. The only potentially hidden element would be王小二's identity as an immortal (仙人), but this is stated openly in dialogue/description ('拖一个仙人下水') and is visible to all present. No fact meets the criteria for a hidden/future-known/player-visibility fact.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

The seed shows the Four Lechers of Central Continent capturing and preparing to assault female Gu Masters, but their motives are straightforward (lechery/evil behavior) and already described by their identity as 'four lechers'. The seed does not provide enough context to infer a deeper or more specific motive beyond what is already visible. Any inferred motive would be a psychological leap or redundant with observed behavior.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

insufficient source-backed seed: The seed focuses on individual actors (中洲四大淫贼 as four named individuals, 王小二 as an individual仙人, 赵怜云 as individual savior, 女蛊师们 as victims). The only collective actor mentioned is '中洲四大淫贼' (Four Great Lechers of Central Continent), but they are a loose group of four named individuals acting together in this chapter, not a persistent faction/clan/organization with ongoing territorial or resource stakes. The seed describes a single encounter (battle, capture, attempted assault, then punishment by Zhao Lianyun) with no indication of lasting faction-level pressures, territorial interests, succession concerns, or resource competition. The '中洲正道蛊师' are mentioned only as defeated opponents in one battle, not as an organized faction with ongoing pressure. No family, clan, shop, hall, or named organization appears with a concrete stake beyond this single incident. Rejecting as individual-only pressure subjects.
IF export filter quarantined 4 legacy old-schema branches.
